structured.title: Rachel and Fed Clarify Church Stance
structured.overview: Rachel and Fed discussed a sensitive church leadership issue involving Chloe’s baby shower for Sophie and Sarah, a same-sex couple recently welcomed into the church. Rachel explained that the public invite appears to have been taken as an endorsement of the relationship, which has upset some members who hold a traditional theological view of marriage. Fed agreed the church should remain loving and welcoming while clearly stating its doctrinal position, and Rachel planned to speak with Chloe first to reduce fallout before addressing Sophie and Sarah if needed. They also touched on family dynamics, Rachel’s theology studies, recent health concerns in the family, and some travel plans, but the main focus was how to handle the church conflict with sensitivity and clarity.

## Church position on baby shower
- Chloe’s WhatsApp baby shower caused uproar.
- Church warm welcome was seen as endorsement.
- Many messages asked church’s position.

## Biblical conviction discussed
- Marriage affirmed as man and woman.
- Same-sex relationship support remains pastoral, not endorsing.
- Context matters, but Scripture stays final.

## Family tensions explained
- Steve’s family already knows his stance.
- Chloe and Rana are more emotive.
- Sensitivity needed to avoid arguments.

## How to handle it
- Steve should speak with Chloe first.
- Message should clarify theology and sensitivity.
- Pastoral goal: keep love, reduce fallout.

## Wider church concerns
- Some asked about serving roles.
- Baby shower invited the whole church.
- Concern: welcome should not equal approval.

## Next steps
- Steve to break the ice with Chloe.
- Clarify church position to Sophie and Sarah.
- Keep conversations respectful and brief.
